description:
## Background

`bsm-kia.12` requires the typed harness parser to exist as exported
symbols before any consumer can switch to it. Without this expand
step the migrate sub-issues would have to introduce the parser and
swap every consumer in one commit, breaking the expand–contract
pattern.

## What to build

A typed parser for harness inputs that produces a fully validated
`HarnessInputs` object from `process.env`, exported from
`e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts` (or a sibling helper
module).

## Technical Approach

- Define `type Scenario = "empty" | "issues" | "atomic-switch"` and
  `type Phase = "1" | "2"` (the only phases currently used).
- Define
  `interface HarnessInputs { scenario: Scenario; phase: Phase; fixtureA: string; fixtureB?: string; fixtureBSecond?: string; storePath: string }`.
- Implement and export `isScenario`, `isPhase`, `parseScenario`,
  `parsePhase`, and `parseHarnessEnvironment`.
- `parseHarnessEnvironment(env)` returns the typed object or throws
  one consolidated error listing every missing/invalid input.
- Do not touch the existing `process.env.*` reads yet; that
  happens in `bsm-kia.12.2` through `bsm-kia.12.4`.

## Considerations

- The error message must list every missing input so users see all
  failures at once, not one error per `if`.
- Don't introduce a real option parser (commander/yargs) — that is
  out of scope.
- Don't rename env vars; only the consumption side becomes typed.
  The launcher CLI surface (positional args) stays unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts` (or sibling helper)
      exports `Scenario`, `Phase`, `HarnessInputs`, `isScenario`,
      `isPhase`, `parseScenario`, `parsePhase`, and
      `parseHarnessEnvironment`.
- [ ] No spec or wdio config imports the new symbols yet (this is
      the expand step).
- [ ] Existing `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and
      `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` still pass with no behavioral
      change.

## Test Plan

- Add a Vitest unit test file (e.g.,
  `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.test.ts`) that verifies:
  - `parseScenario` accepts each of the three valid strings and
    throws on unknown.
  - `parsePhase` accepts `"1"` and `"2"` and throws on anything
    else.
  - `parseHarnessEnvironment` returns a complete object when every
    variable is present.
  - `parseHarnessEnvironment` throws a single consolidated error
    that names every missing variable.
- Run `pnpm test`, `pnpm exec ultracite check`, and
  `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` to prove no regression.
title: Expand: add typed harness parser in run-scenario.ts
